translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word of Jehovah that came to Joel the son of Pethuel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear this, ye old men, and give ear, all ye inhabitants of the land. Hath this been in your days, or even in the days of your fathers?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Tell your children of it, and [let] your children [tell] their children, and their children another generation:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that which the palmer-worm hath left hath the locust eaten; and that which the locust hath left hath the cankerworm eaten; and that which the cankerworm hath left hath the caterpillar eaten.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Awake, ye drunkards, and weep; and howl, all ye drinkers of wine, because of the new wine: for it is cut off from your mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For a nation is come up upon my land, strong and without number: his teeth are the teeth of a lion, and he hath the cheek teeth of a lioness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath made my vine a desolation, and barked my fig-tree; he hath made it clean bare, and cast it away: its branches are made white.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wail like a virgin girded with sackcloth for the husband of her youth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The oblation and the drink-offering are cut off from the house of Jehovah; the priests, Jehovah's ministers, mourn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The field is laid waste, the land mourneth; for the corn is wasted, the new wine is dried up, the oil languisheth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be ashamed, ye husbandmen; howl, ye vinedressers, for the wheat and for the barley: because the harvest of the field hath perished.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The vine is dried up, and the fig-tree languisheth; the pomegranate-tree, the palm also and the apple-tree; all the trees of the field are withered, yea, joy is withered away from the children of men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Gird yourselves, and lament, ye priests; howl, ministers of the altar; come, lie all night in sackcloth, ye ministers of myGod: for the oblation and the drink-offering are withholden from the house of yourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hallow a fast, proclaim a solemn assembly, gather the elders, [and] all the inhabitants of the land to the house of Jehovah yourGod, and cry unto Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Alas for the day! for the day of Jehovah is at hand, and as destruction from the Almighty shall it come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is not the food cut off before our eyes, joy and gladness from the house of ourGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The seeds are rotten under their clods, the granaries are laid desolate, the barns are broken down; for the corn is withered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How do the beasts groan! The herds of cattle are bewildered, for they have no pasture; the flocks of sheep also are in suffering.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To thee, Jehovah, do I cry; for the fire hath devoured the pastures of the wilderness, and the flame hath burned up all the trees of the field.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The beasts of the field also cry unto thee; for the water-courses are dried, and the fire hath devoured the pastures of the wilderness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blow the trumpet in Zion, and sound an alarm in my holy mountain; let all the inhabitants of the land tremble: for the day of Jehovah cometh, for it is at hand;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: a day of darkness and gloom, a day of clouds and gross darkness, as the dawn spread upon the mountains; — a great people and a strong; there hath not been ever the like, neither shall be any more after them, to the years of generations and generations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A fire devoureth before them, and behind them a flame burneth; the land is as a garden of Eden before them, and behind them a desolate wilderness: yea, and nothing escapeth them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The appearance of them is as the appearance of horses; and as horsemen, so they run.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Like the noise of chariots, on the tops of the mountains, they leap; like the noise of a flame of fire that devoureth the stubble, as a strong people set in battle array.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Before them the peoples are in anguish: all faces turn pale.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They run like mighty men; they climb the wall like men of war; and they march every one on his ways, and break not their ranks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Neither doth one press upon another; they march every one in his path; and fall amid weapons, but are not wounded.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They spread themselves over the city; they run upon the wall; they climb up into the houses; they enter in by the windows like a thief.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The earth quaketh before them; the heavens tremble; the sun and the moon are darkened, and the stars withdraw their shining.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah uttereth his voice before his army; for his camp is very great; for strong is he that executeth his word: for the day of Jehovah is great and very terrible; and who can bear it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yet even now, saith Jehovah, turn to me with all your heart, and with fasting, and with weeping, and with mourning;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and rend your heart, and not your garments, and turn unto Jehovah yourGod: for he is gracious and merciful, slow to anger, and of great loving-kindness, and repenteth him of the evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who knoweth? He might return and repent, and leave a blessing behind him, an oblation and a drink-offering for Jehovah yourGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blow the trumpet in Zion, hallow a fast, proclaim a solemn assembly;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: gather the people, hallow the congregation, assemble the elders, gather the children, and those that suck the breasts; let the bridegroom go forth from his chamber, and the bride from her closet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the priests, the ministers of Jehovah, weep between the porch and the altar, and let them say, Spare, O Jehovah, thy people, and give not thine inheritance to reproach, that they should be a byword of the nations. Wherefore should they say among the peoples, Where is theirGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Jehovah will be jealous for his land, and will have pity on his people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah will answer and say unto his people, Behold, I send you corn, and new wine, and oil, and ye shall be satisfied therewith; and I will no more make you a reproach among the nations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will remove far off from you him [that cometh] from the north, and will drive him into a land barren and desolate, his face toward the eastern sea, and his rear toward the hinder sea; and his stench shall come up, and his ill odour shall come up, for he hath exalted himself to do great things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — Fear not, O land; be glad and rejoice: for Jehovah doeth great things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not afraid, ye beasts of the field: for the pastures of the wilderness do spring; for the tree beareth its fruit; the fig-tree and the vine yield full increase.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye, children of Zion, be glad and rejoice in Jehovah yourGod; for he giveth you the early rain in due measure, and he causeth to come down for you the rain, the early rain, and the latter rain at the beginning [of the season].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the floors shall be full of corn, and the vats shall overflow with new wine and oil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will restore to you the years that the locust hath eaten, the cankerworm, and the caterpillar, and the palmer-worm, my great army which I sent among you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall eat in plenty, and be satisfied, and praise the name of Jehovah yourGod, who hath dealt wondrously with you: and my people shall never be ashamed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall know that I am in the midst of Israel, and that I, Jehovah, [am] yourGod, and there is none else: and my people shall never be ashamed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass afterwards [that] I will pour out my Spirit upon all flesh; and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, your old men shall dream dreams, your young men shall see visions.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, even upon the bondmen and upon the handmaids in those days will I pour out my Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will shew wonders in the heavens and on the earth, blood, and fire, and pillars of smoke.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sun shall be changed to darkness, and the moon to blood, before the great and terrible day of Jehovah come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be that whosoever shall call upon the name of Jehovah shall be saved: for in mount Zion and in Jerusalem shall be deliverance, as Jehovah hath said, and for the residue whom Jehovah shall call.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For behold, in those days, and in that time, when I shall turn again the captivity of Judah and Jerusalem,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will also gather all the nations, and will bring them down into the valley of Jehoshaphat, and I will enter into judgment with them there on account of my people and mine inheritance, Israel, whom they have scattered among the nations: and they have parted my land;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they have cast lots for my people, and have given a boy for a harlot, and sold a girl for wine, and have drunk [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea also, what have ye to do with me, O Tyre and Zidon, and all the districts of Philistia? Will ye render me a recompence? But if ye recompense me, swiftly [and] speedily will I bring your recompence upon your own head;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because ye have taken my silver and my gold, and have carried into your temples my beautiful pleasant things,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the children of Judah and the children of Jerusalem have ye sold unto the children of the Greeks, that ye might remove them far from their border.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I will raise them up out of the place whither ye have sold them, and will bring your recompence upon your own head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will sell your sons and your daughters into the hand of the children of Judah, and they shall sell them to the Sabeans, to a nation far off: for Jehovah hath spoken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Proclaim this among the nations: prepare war, arouse the mighty men, let all the men of war draw near, let them come up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Beat your ploughshares into swords, and your pruning-knives into spears; let the weak say, I am strong.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Haste ye and come, all ye nations round about, and gather yourselves together. Thither cause thy mighty ones to come down, O Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the nations rouse themselves, and come up to the valley of Jehoshaphat; for there will I sit to judge all the nations round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Put in the sickle, for the harvest is ripe: come, get you down, for the press is full, the vats overflow; for their wickedness is great.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Multitudes, multitudes in the valley of decision! For the day of Jehovah is at hand in the valley of decision.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sun and the moon shall be darkened, and the stars shall withdraw their shining.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah will roar from Zion, and utter his voice from Jerusalem; and the heavens and the earth shall shake: and Jehovah will be a shelter for his people, and the refuge of the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall know that I, Jehovah, [am] yourGod, dwelling in Zion, my holy mountain; and Jerusalem shall be holy, and no strangers shall pass through her any more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass in that day, that the mountains shall drop down new wine, and the hills shall flow with milk, and all the water-courses of Judah shall flow with waters; and a fountain shall come forth from the house of Jehovah, and shall water the valley of Shittim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Egypt shall be a desolation, and Edom shall be a desolate wilderness, for the violence against the children of Judah, in that they have shed innocent blood in their land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Judah shall abide for ever, and Jerusalem from generation to generation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will purge them from the blood from which I had not purged them: for Jehovah dwelleth in Zion.
